question: Which Garmin-shaped fixture families beyond the required activities, sleep, and body-composition families should Mission 013 include, and which default retention posture should the synthetic future-real-import approval UX present?
context_summary: Public Garmin documentation supports the required activity, sleep/wellness, and body-composition shape. It also publicly lists wellness domains such as HRV, stress, respiration, Body Battery, pulse ox, steps, calories, and heart rate. Mission 013 remains synthetic-only and does not enable real imports.
reason_for_interrupt: Fixture breadth affects implementation size, test coverage, workbench catalog shape, and evidence value. Retention default affects the synthetic approval UX and future real-data bridge posture.

options.label: Add wellness/HRV/stress; keep raw until verified
options.effect: Adds a compact wellness family covering HRV/stress-style rows beyond the required three and presents `keep-raw-until-verified` as the default retention posture for future real imports.
options.risk: Adds moderate fixture and adapter surface area, but keeps retention conservative and aligned with Mission 012's short/local raw-data posture.

options.label: Required families only; keep normalized only
options.effect: Limits fixtures to activities, sleep, and body composition, and presents `keep-normalized-only` as the default future retention posture.
options.risk: Lower implementation size, but less evidence for Garmin wellness categories and less ability to re-preview raw rows after verification.

options.label: Add wellness plus hydration; keep raw and normalized
options.effect: Adds wellness/HRV/stress plus hydration-shaped synthetic rows and presents `keep-raw-and-normalized` as the default future retention posture.
options.risk: Broadest evidence surface, but hydration was lower-confidence in the public research set and raw retention is the least conservative future real-data default.

asked.message_reference: Mission 013 ChatGPT thread; sponsor asked to answer from phone via Codex mobile app if possible.
